topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The delivery notification blinked on my phone, a stark, unwelcome intrusion into my quiet afternoon. A package. Addressed to the old unit, 3B. I felt a slow, cold tightening in my chest, a familiar surge of righteous irritation. It was the remnants of their tenancy, some forgotten junk I’d been hoping to discard for months. I picked up the box, the cardboard rough beneath my fingertips, and carried it up the stairs with deliberate slowness. When I finally got it to my doorstep, I didn't just leave it; I placed it squarely in the center of the welcome mat, making sure it was impossible to miss. I watched the street, waiting for the inevitable discovery, a small, tight smile playing on my lips as I contemplated the small, satisfying disruption I had orchestrated.

[story 2]

My jaw clenched so hard I thought my teeth might crack. The courier had dropped off the parcel addressed to the previous occupants of this apartment. It was a deliberate act, I knew it was. A calculated move to cause a stir. I retrieved the package, the weight of it feeling significant in my hand, and retreated to my room. I didn't open it immediately. Instead, I paced the length of the floor, my boots clicking sharply on the hardwood. Every nerve ending was buzzing with a restless energy. I imagined the look on their face when they realized what had happened, a fleeting, unpleasant image that fueled a sharp, internal satisfaction. I made a mental note to ensure the contents were displayed prominently, a silent declaration of my displeasure.

[story 3]

When the doorbell rang, I hesitated for only a second before opening the door. There, sitting on the threshold, was the package meant for the folks who used to live here. My gaze swept over it—a small, unassuming box—and a slow, predatory gleam entered my eyes. I didn't bother to ask who it was for. Instead, I took the item inside, setting it down on the kitchen counter with a deliberate, almost theatrical placement. I leaned against the counter, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, a look of supreme self-satisfaction washing over my features. The silence in the apartment seemed to amplify the quiet triumph blooming inside me.
